title: TC-DFU-VER-001: Home Screen firmware version stale after DFU — no app restart
steps: Precondition: Device on firmware v1.0.0. App connected.
Trigger DFU to v2.0.0 via: Device → Software & Updates → Update banner.
Wait for DFU completion (device restarts, app shows reconnected state).
DO NOT restart the app. Observe Home screen firmware version string below device name.
Navigate to: Device → [Device Name] → observe firmware version at top of screen.
Return to Home screen. Observe firmware version again.
expected: Expected: Home screen shows v2.0.0. Device Settings page shows v2.0.0. Actual (If Bug): Home screen and Device Settings page both show stale v1.0.0. The version string does not update until app is force-killed and relaunched.

title: TC-DFU-VER-002: Home Screen firmware version correct after DFU + app restart
steps: Precondition: Device on firmware v1.0.0. App connected.
Trigger DFU to v2.0.0 via: Device → Software & Updates → Update banner.
Wait for DFU completion (device restarts, app shows reconnected state).
Force-kill the app (swipe from recents / app settings → Force Stop).
Relaunch the app. Wait for device reconnection.
Observe Home screen firmware version string below device name.
Navigate to: Device → [Device Name] → observe firmware version at top of screen.
expected: Expected: Home screen shows v2.0.0. Device Settings page shows v2.0.0. The version string correctly reflects the post-DFU firmware after app restart.

title: TC-DFU-VER-003: Immediate state trigger — firmware version check before any navigation after DFU
steps: Precondition: Device on firmware v1.0.0. App connected.
Trigger DFU to v2.0.0 via: Device → Software & Updates → Update banner.
Wait for DFU completion (device restarts, app shows reconnected state).
IMMEDIATELY (within 1 second of reconnection) observe Home screen firmware version. Do NOT navigate away or tap anything.
Force-kill the app without any further interaction.
Relaunch app. Observe Home screen firmware version.
expected: Expected (Immediate check): Home screen shows v2.0.0 immediately after DFU reconnection. Expected (After restart): Home screen shows v2.0.0. Actual (If Bug): Immediate check shows stale v1.0.0; only after restart does v2.0.0 appear. This confirms the in-memory cache is not invalidated on DFU completion event.

title: TC-DFU-VER-004: Deep lifecycle — firmware version after DFU + Bluetooth disconnect/reconnect cycle
steps: Precondition: Device on firmware v1.0.0. App connected.
Trigger DFU to v2.0.0 via: Device → Software & Updates → Update banner.
Wait for DFU completion (device restarts, app shows reconnected state).
Without restarting the app: disconnect Bluetooth from phone Settings.
Wait 10 seconds. Reconnect Bluetooth from phone Settings.
Wait for app to show reconnected state.
Observe Home screen firmware version.
Navigate to: Device → [Device Name] → observe firmware version.
Force-kill the app. Relaunch. Observe firmware version on Home screen and Device Settings.
expected: Expected (Before restart): Home screen and Device Settings show v2.0.0 after Bluetooth reconnect. Expected (After restart): v2.0.0 persists. Actual (If Bug): Before restart, stale v1.0.0 persists even through Bluetooth disconnect/reconnect — cache is only cleared on process restart.

title: TC-DFU-VER-005: Deep lifecycle — firmware version after DFU + phone reboot
steps: Precondition: Device on firmware v1.0.0. App connected.
Trigger DFU to v2.0.0 via: Device → Software & Updates → Update banner.
Wait for DFU completion (device restarts, app shows reconnected state).
Without restarting the app: reboot the phone.
After phone boots: launch the app. Wait for device reconnection.
Observe Home screen firmware version.
Navigate to: Device → [Device Name] → observe firmware version.
expected: Expected: Home screen and Device Settings show v2.0.0 after phone reboot + app launch. Actual (If Bug): Stale v1.0.0 appears because the app process was killed by reboot and restarted — but if the cached version is persisted to disk (SharedPreferences/DB) and not invalidated on DFU, even a phone reboot may show stale data. This test validates whether the cache is stored persistently or only in-memory.

title: TC-DFU-VER-006: Firmware version on Device Settings page — immediate check after DFU without navigating away
steps: Precondition: Device on firmware v1.0.0. App connected.
Trigger DFU to v2.0.0 via: Device → Software & Updates → Update banner.
Wait for DFU completion (device restarts, app shows reconnected state).
IMMEDIATELY tap: Device → [Device Name]. Observe firmware version at top of screen.
Return to Home screen. Observe firmware version.
Force-kill app. Relaunch. Check both locations again.
expected: Expected (Immediate): Device Settings page shows v2.0.0, Home screen shows v2.0.0. Expected (After restart): Both show v2.0.0. Actual (If Bug): Device Settings page shows stale v1.0.0 immediately after DFU; Home screen also shows v1.0.0. After restart, both correctly show v2.0.0.

title: TC-DFU-VER-007: Firmware version persistence across app backgrounding (no kill) after DFU
steps: Precondition: Device on firmware v1.0.0. App connected.
Trigger DFU to v2.0.0 via: Device → Software & Updates → Update banner.
Wait for DFU completion (device restarts, app shows reconnected state).
Press Home button to background the app.
Wait 30 seconds. Reopen app from recents (no swipe-kill).
Observe Home screen firmware version.
Navigate to: Device → [Device Name] → observe firmware version.
expected: Expected: Home screen and Device Settings show v2.0.0 after app is backgrounded and resumed. Actual (If Bug): Stale v1.0.0 persists because the in-memory cache survives backgrounding but was never refreshed from the device post-DFU.

title: TC-DFU-VER-008: Firmware version after DFU — app update notification banner dismissal and re-check
steps: Precondition: Device on firmware v1.0.0. App connected.
Trigger DFU to v2.0.0 via: Device → Software & Updates → Update banner.
Wait for DFU completion (device restarts, app shows reconnected state).
Observe the firmware update notification banner (if any) on Home screen.
Dismiss the banner if present.
Observe Home screen firmware version.
Navigate to: Device → [Device Name] → observe firmware version.
Navigate to: Device → Software & Updates → observe 'Current Version' field.
Force-kill app. Relaunch. Repeat all observations.
expected: Expected (Before restart): Home screen shows v2.0.0, Device Settings shows v2.0.0, Software & Updates shows v2.0.0. Expected (After restart): All locations show v2.0.0. Actual (If Bug): Before restart, all locations show stale v1.0.0 despite DFU completion. The 'Current Version' field in Software & Updates may also show stale data. After restart, all correctly show v2.0.0.
